teamObjective.explanation: I hold only two cards and the scenario is an endgame race; the immediate priority is to empty my own hand as quickly as possible while preserving a chance to win the lead back.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner hand composition is unknown. I only observe that my partner has three cards remaining and no public cards have been played.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent hands are unknown. Both opponents hold three cards each and may contain trump 7s or higher cards that could beat my spade Ace.
evidence: handCounts shows opponents with 3 cards each
trumpRank is 7, so any 7 is higher than non-trump Ace

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the Ace of spades maximizes the chance of winning this trick. If I win the lead, I can immediately play the deuce on the next lead and finish my hand in one round.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leading the deuce is weaker; it is far more likely to be beaten by any higher card or trump, leaving me with only the Ace and no guaranteed opportunity to play it.

risks: An opponent or partner may play a trump 7 or higher card and win the trick, leaving me with the deuce.
Even if I win with the Ace, a subsequent lead of the deuce could still be beaten, delaying my finish.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Playing the Ace first is the highest-probability path to retain the lead and complete the hand; no alternative sequencing improves this given only two singletons.

notes: Reasoning based solely on the visible hand and public counts; no hidden cards are asserted as facts.
